title: alyssum campestre proles fontqueri
intro: Alyssum campestre proles fontqueri, also known as Fontquer's alyssum, is a subspecies of the alyssum plant, a low-growing, annual or biennial herb with delicate white or pink flowers. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, although its effectiveness and safety as a treatment have not been extensively scientifically studied.
